m_Item1.QuestionText:
What is the functionality of the following code?
public void function(Node node)
{
    if(size == 0)
        head = node;
    else
    {
        Node temp,cur;
        for(cur = head; (temp = cur.getNext())!=null; cur = temp);
        cur.setNext(node);
    }
    size++;
}

Options.OptionText: Inserting a node at the beginning of the list.
Deleting a node at the beginning of the list
Inserting a node at the end of the list
Deleting a node at the end of the list

m_Item1.Explanation: The for loop traverses through the list and then inserts a new node as cur.setNext(node);

m_Item1.QuestionText: What should be added in place of ---------------------------, so that the function correctly reverses a linked list.
m_Item1.Explanation: At the end of while loop, the prev pointer points to the last node of original linked list. We need to change *head_ref so that the head pointer now starts pointing to the last node. See the following complete running program.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Code:
void freeList( struct node *n )
{
 while( n )
 {
  ????
 }
}
Which one of the following can replace the ???? for the function above to release the memory allocated to a linked list?

Options.OptionText:
struct node m = n;
n = n->next;
free( m );

struct node m = n;
free( n );
n = m->next;

m_Item1.Explanation: To release memory allocated for list, we have to iterate through each node of list till the end of list i.e.up to NULL & free every single node.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In a circular linked list, following is true

Options.OptionText: Components are all linked together in some sequential manner.
There is no beginning and no end.
Forward and backward traversal within the list is permitted

m_Item1.Explanation: In circular linked list last node will always point to first node, so no starting & end of list, but we can use some pointer that will be pointing to first & last node.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In what kind of storage we can easily insert,delete,concatenate and rearrange substrings ?
m_Item1.Explanation: linked list are those who can grow/shrink dynamically as per the requirement.
